surfaces.name: technique-section-lift
surfaces.summary: Lifts stable source sections into bounded KAG-friendly section units without replacing authored markdown meaning.

surfaces.name: metadata-spine-projection
surfaces.summary: Projects bounded source metadata into a KAG-friendly identity and routing spine without replacing source meaning.

surfaces.name: bounded-relation-view
surfaces.summary: Lifts direct typed relations into bounded edge hints without implying full graph semantics.

surfaces.name: provenance-note-view
surfaces.summary: Lifts provenance-oriented note handles into bounded KAG surfaces that preserve source linkage.

surfaces.name: tos-text-chunk-map
surfaces.summary: Maps authored ToS texts into bounded chunk-level retrieval units with explicit source linkage.

surfaces.name: cross-source-node-projection
surfaces.summary: Creates a bounded one-hop cross-source node projection from source-owned exports while preserving explicit primary versus supporting provenance.

surfaces.name: tos-retrieval-axis-surface
surfaces.summary: Returns bounded lineage, conflict, practice, and source handles for one ToS chunk set as a bridge-ready retrieval surface for AoA consumers.

surfaces.name: counterpart-edge-view
surfaces.summary: Projects bounded non-identity counterpart edges from ToS concepts into selected AoA operational surfaces without granting graph sovereignty.

surfaces.name: federation-readiness-spine
surfaces.summary: Materializes a bounded federation entry spine from current source-owned tiny KAG exports in aoa-techniques and Tree-of-Sophia without claiming a wider completed federation.

surfaces.name: tos-zarathustra-route-pack
surfaces.summary: Projects the full canonical Zarathustra prologue-1 route into one bounded KAG-ready bundle of route-local nodes and canonical relations without replacing Tree-of-Sophia authored tree surfaces.

surfaces.name: tos-zarathustra-route-retrieval-surface
surfaces.summary: Returns bounded family-level handles for the canonical Zarathustra prologue-1 route bundle so consumers can traverse canonical ToS route surfaces without ranking, source replacement, or routing ownership.
